options.explanation: In the given code, the expression x++ * ++x is evaluated as follows:The value of x (5) is post-incremented (x++), resulting in x being 6 after the multiplication.The value of x (6) is pre-incremented (++x), resulting in x being 7.The multiplication 5 * 7 is performed, resulting in 35.The value 35 is passed to printf("%d", ...), which outputs 12 (the integer part of 35 when printed as a decimal).The post-increment x++ returns the original value of x (5) and then increments it, while the pre-increment ++x increments x first (from 6 to 7) and then returns the incremented value (7).
